task_completion.description: Open the return_journey.drawio file in a diagramming application
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io or diagrams.net interface is visible with the return journey map scaffold loaded.

task_completion.description: Fill all 25 content cells in the grid
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 5x5 grid (5 stages across 5 rows: Touchpoints, Actions, Emotional States, Pain Points, Opportunities) is fully populated with text.
The cells in the 'Emotional States' row have background colors applied: light green for positive, light yellow for neutral, and light red for negative.

task_completion.description: Add at least 5 dependency arrows connecting Pain Points to Opportunities
task_completion.visual_evidence: There are at least 5 visible arrows or connector lines specifically linking cells in the 'Pain Points' row to corresponding cells in the 'Opportunities' row.

task_completion.description: Export the completed map as a PNG to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog saving the file as 'return_journey.png' to '~/Diagrams/exports/', or the exported file visible in a file manager or terminal listing.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical interface to edit the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the draw.io/diagrams.net GUI tools (e.g., text editing, color picker, connector tools) rather than editing XML via a text editor or script.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's export functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the File -> Export as -> PNG menu or dialog being used, rather than a command-line conversion tool.

integrity.description: The diagram content reflects the provided specification
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the diagram contains e-commerce return terminology (e.g., 'return policy', 'restocking', 'order history') and is not generic placeholder text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 5 stages (columns) are: Discover Return Policy, Initiate Return, Prepare & Ship, Await Processing, Receive Resolution. Colors for Emotional States: Positive = #d5e8d4 (light green), Neutral = #fff2cc (light yellow), Negative = #f8cecc (light red). Key phrases to look for in the populated text: 'return policy', 'restocking', 'order history', '67%', '92%', 'baymard', 'narvar'. The final export must be saved exactly to ~/Diagrams/exports/return_journey.png.
